rename.title: Build file name
rename.description: Combine text and metadata to build the file name.

export.title: Export collection
export.subtitle: Surco saves a collection file. Your DJ software reads it as an import source — nothing is added to its library automatically; you import the tracks from there.
export.rekordboxHint: In rekordbox: Preferences → Advanced → rekordbox xml → pick this file, then import from the “rekordbox xml” node.
export.traktorHint: In Traktor: File → Import Collection → pick this file.
export.seratoHint: In Serato: drop the saved file into your _Serato_/Subcrates folder, then restart Serato — your tracks show up in the sidebar.
export.m3uHint: A plain playlist for everything else — players, car units, other library tools.
export.unconverted: {{count}} track(s) aren’t converted yet — they’ll be exported as their original files.

header.convertSelectedEmptyHint: Select tracks in the list to convert them
header.fillFromName: Fill all tags from file name
header.autoMatchNoToken: Add a Discogs token in Settings to auto-match

sidebar.dropHint: Drag your files here, or press “Add files”.
sidebar.fitHint: Double-click to fit the width to the track names

filter.label: Filter
filter.trashSuspects_one: Move the suspect file to Trash
filter.trashSuspects_other: Move the {{count}} suspect files to Trash

empty.title: Add tracks to get started.
empty.subtitle: Converts your tracks to lossless AIFF, tags them from Discogs and sends them to Apple Music.
empty.subtitleNoMusic: Converts your tracks to lossless AIFF and tags them from Discogs.

errors.settingsLoad: Your settings couldn't be loaded — defaults are in use for this session.

notices.normalizeSkipped: Couldn't measure the loudness of “{{name}}” — converted without normalization.
notices.metaReadFailed_one: Couldn't read the tags of {{count}} file — showing its file name only.
notices.metaReadFailed_other: Couldn't read the tags of {{count}} files — showing their file names only.

player.continuousHelp: Continuous playback: when a track ends, the next one in the list plays automatically.
player.waveformHelp: Show the waveform. Hiding it skips the waveform analysis — useful for large files or network drives.
player.reveal: Show the playing track in the list

commands.fillAll: Fill all tags from file name

trackList.label: Tracks
trackList.metaReadFailed: Couldn't read this file's tags — showing file name data only

editor.searchPlaceholder: Search Discogs or paste a release id…
editor.tokenTip: Search works out of the box. Add your own Discogs token in <0>Settings</0> for a higher, private rate limit.
editor.chooseAlbumHint: Choose an album from the list to see its tracks.
editor.fitHint: Double-click to fit the width to the results
editor.coverDrop: Drag or click to add artwork
editor.coverTitle: Drag an image to use it as the artwork
editor.qualityCaptionGood: Energy reaches ~{{cutoff}}, at the ~20 kHz full-quality line.
editor.qualityCaptionWarn: Energy reaches ~{{cutoff}}, short of the ~20 kHz full-quality line.
editor.qualityCaptionBad: Ceiling at ~{{cutoff}}, the mark of a lossy source.
editor.qualityCaptionProcessed: Highs that rise where a natural spectrum falls — regenerated over a real ceiling near ~{{cutoff}}.
editor.qualityUpsampled: Declared above 44.1 kHz, but the real content walls off at 22.05 kHz — upsampled from 44.1 kHz, not true hi-res.
editor.qualityCaptionGenuine: No codec cut: tapers smoothly to ~{{cutoff}}, a gently rolled-off but genuine master.
editor.qualityCaptionTranscode: A codec cut at ~{{cutoff}} inside a lossless container: a lossy source repackaged.
editor.loudnessLufsHint: Integrated loudness (LUFS) — how loud the track sounds overall
editor.loudnessPeakHint: True peak (dBTP) — the loudest instant of the signal
editor.loudnessRangeHint: Loudness range (LU) — the spread between quiet and loud parts
editor.loudnessCrestHint: Crest factor (dB) — the punch between peaks and the average level
editor.loudnessBalanceHint: Channel balance (dB) — the level difference between left and right
editor.loudnessDcHint: DC offset — a constant bias that shifts the waveform off centre
editor.loudnessNoiseHint: Noise floor (dB) — the background level; lower is cleaner
editor.loudnessLufsHelp: (LUFS) — how loud the track sounds overall. Too low and it plays quieter than your other tracks; too high and it sounds crushed.
editor.loudnessPeakHelp: (dBTP) — the loudest instant of the signal. Above 0 it can distort (clip) when converted or played back.
editor.loudnessRangeHelp: (LU) — how much the quiet and loud parts differ. A very low range means heavy compression (the loudness war).
editor.loudnessCrestHelp: (dB) — the gap between the peaks and the average level, i.e. the punch. A low figure means the track is squashed flat.
editor.loudnessBalanceHelp: (L/R) — the level difference between the left and right channels. A few dB means one side is weaker, often a misaligned cartridge on the rip.
editor.loudnessDcHelp: — a constant bias that shifts the whole waveform off centre. It wastes headroom and can add clicks; near 0% is healthy.
editor.loudnessNoiseHelp: (dB) — the level of the background hiss/noise. The lower (more negative), the cleaner the capture.
editor.outputNameHintInPlace: Same format as the source: this updates the original file in place and renames it — no copy is written to the output folder.
editor.overwriteHint: This rewrites the source file in place, keeping its name. No copy goes to the output folder. Turn this off in Settings → Conversion.
editor.overwriteLossyHint: This replaces the lossless original with a 320 kbps MP3 in place — the master is lost and can't be recovered. Turn this off in Settings → Conversion.
editor.regenerateHint: Rebuild the file name from the metadata above using your Settings → Naming pattern
editor.regenerateCustom: Customize the filename pattern for this track…
editor.appleMusicError: Could not add to Apple Music
editor.inLibrary: Already in your Apple Music library
editor.inLibraryEngine: Already in your Engine DJ library
editor.notInLibrary: Not in your Apple Music library
editor.notInLibraryEngine: Not in your Engine DJ library
editor.deriveFromNameHint: Fill the metadata fields from the file name
editor.copyFilenameHint: Copy the file name to the clipboard

normalize.title: Loudness normalization
normalize.hint: Off by default. Match every track to a target loudness or peak. It re-encodes the audio, so it drops Traktor cue/beatgrid markers.
normalize.cueWarning: Re-encodes the audio. Traktor cues and beatgrid are kept for MP3 and AIFF, but dropped for WAV and FLAC.

shortcuts.intro: Click a shortcut, then press the new keys. Esc cancels.
shortcuts.conflict: Two commands share a shortcut. Resolve it to save.
shortcuts.fixedNav: Always on (not rebindable): ↑↓ or j / k move through the track list and the Discogs results, Home / End jump to the ends, and Page Up / Down move a page at a time.

stats.perTrack: ≈ {{minutes}} min of manual work per track
stats.empty: Nothing converted yet. Once you start, you'll see how much time Surco saves you here.
stats.donate: If Surco saves you hours, support its development

settings.configDirHint: Pick a folder in iCloud Drive or Dropbox to share these preferences across your Macs. Applies immediately; the Discogs token and this Mac's stats never leave this machine.
settings.cacheHint: Spectrograms and quality checks are saved to disk so reopening your library is instant. {{count}} files · {{size}}. Emptying it just makes the next open recompute them.
settings.cacheHintEmpty: Spectrograms and quality checks are saved to disk so reopening your library is instant. Nothing cached yet.
settings.discogsDisabledHint: Enable Discogs above to configure these.
settings.tokenWhy: Optional. Search uses shared credentials that can briefly fail at peak times; your own token keeps it fast.
settings.autoMatchHint: On import, search your sources and apply confident matches. Discogs leads, Bandcamp falls back. Off by default.
settings.autoMatchNeedsToken: Discogs is one of your sources — add its token below to auto-match.
settings.autoMatchNeedsSource: Enable at least one search source to auto-match.
settings.discogsFormatsHint: Limit Discogs results to these formats.
settings.maxResultsHint: How many search results to show. Fewer keeps the right release on screen instead of a long tail of compilations and reissues.
settings.searchProvidersHint: Catalogs the editor searches. Discogs is curated; Bandcamp adds self-released material.
settings.outputFormatHint: AIFF/WAV/FLAC are lossless (FLAC compressed); MP3 is smaller. A file already in the format is copied without re-encoding.
settings.mp3QualityHint: Applies to every MP3 export. 320 kbps is the DJ-pool standard; V0 is smaller at transparent quality.
settings.destinationAppleMusicHint: Adds to Apple Music, no output-folder copy.
settings.destinationEngineDjHint: Registers it in your Engine DJ library; the file stays in the output folder.
settings.destinationOverwriteHint: Rewrites each source file in place; no output copy. Changing format removes the original.
settings.engineLibraryDirHint: Surco writes into this library's database. Close Engine DJ while converting.
settings.engineDjPlaylistHint: Converted tracks are added to this playlist in your library, on top of the collection.
settings.appleMusicFlacNote: Apple Music can't play FLAC, so converted tracks stay in the output folder while FLAC is your format.
settings.autoApplyFilenameHint: Builds the name from metadata on export, without pressing "Regenerate". A manual edit always wins.
settings.filenameFolderHint: Use "/" to sort files into folders, e.g.
settings.groupingHint: Shown as quick buttons when editing a track.
settings.genreHint: Shown as quick buttons alongside the ones from Discogs.
settings.coverHint: Applied on embed: shrinks large artwork and, if enabled, center-crops it to square.
settings.replaceLowRes: Replace low-res covers when applying a release
settings.replaceLowResHint: When off, your file's own cover is always kept — a low-res one is only flagged, never swapped for the release image.
settings.showSpectrumHint: Shows the spectrum and quality check while editing a track. When off, the analysis is skipped entirely.
settings.autoAnalyzeHint: Queues every imported track's quality analysis in the background, so suspect files get flagged without pressing Analyze.
settings.showLoudnessHint: Measures integrated loudness, true peak and loudness range (EBU R128) while editing a track. When off, the measurement is skipped entirely.
settings.keyNotationHint: How the detected key suggestion is shown under the Key field. Camelot is what DJ software sorts by.

palette.placeholder: Search tracks or run a command…

onboarding.welcomeBody: Let's set up the essentials. It takes less than a minute, and you can change everything later in Settings.
onboarding.tokenBody: Surco tags your tracks with data from Discogs and works without any setup. Optionally paste a free personal token for your own, higher rate limit.
onboarding.autoMatchBody: Searches Discogs for every file you drop and fills confident matches automatically — this uses a lot of requests.
onboarding.autoMatchNeedsToken: Add a token above to enable auto-match.
onboarding.formatBody: Choose the format Surco exports your tracks to.
onboarding.namingBody: Decide whether the name is built from metadata automatically or regenerated by you with a button. You can tweak the pattern in Settings → Naming.
onboarding.groupingBody: Optional. Add your own labels to tag tracks faster while editing.
onboarding.genreBody: Optional. Add your usual genres so they're one click away even when a release isn't on Discogs.
onboarding.fieldsBody: Choose which fields the editor shows (and their order), and which must be filled before a track can be converted. Title and artist are the usual minimum; you can change all of this later in Settings.
onboarding.spectrumBody: Surco can show a spectrum of each track to spot lossy files re-encoded as lossless. If you'd rather not see it, turn it off — the analysis is then skipped entirely. You can change this later in Settings.

token.q: Do I need a Discogs token?
token.a:
No. Surco searches Discogs out of the box using its own app credentials, so you can start tagging right away.

Those shared credentials have a combined rate limit (60 requests/minute across all Surco users), so during busy moments a search may occasionally fail with a “rate limit reached” message — just wait a few seconds and try again.

If you tag a lot, generate your own free personal token at discogs.com/settings/developers and paste it in Settings → General. Your token gets its own private rate limit, so searches stay fast no matter how busy Surco is.

quality.q: Why is a track flagged for “Review”?
quality.a:
Surco analyzes the audio spectrum and checks how high the frequencies reach. Genuine lossless files (WAV, FLAC, AIFF) use the full range up to the Nyquist frequency. A sharp cutoff well below it is the fingerprint of a lossy MP3 that was re-encoded into a lossless container: it looks lossless by its extension, but the high frequencies are gone for good.

“Review” means that fingerprint was detected and is worth a listen: the file isn't broken, but its quality may be capped by an original MP3 (or it's simply a dull master). “Good quality” means the spectrum reaches where a real lossless file should.

format.q: Which output format should I choose?
format.a:
AIFF and WAV are lossless — they keep the exact original quality and carry tags cleanly, ideal for DJing. AIFF tends to handle metadata and artwork best on Apple platforms.

MP3 (320 kbps) is lossy but takes far less space — handy when storage or USB size matters.

Whichever you pick, a file already in that format is copied without re-encoding, so you never lose quality to a needless conversion.

appleMusic.q: What does “Add to Apple Music” do?
appleMusic.a:
When enabled (macOS only), Surco automatically imports each converted track into your Apple Music library right after processing, so your freshly tagged files land there without a manual drag-and-drop.

While editing, Surco also checks whether a track is already in your library and shows a small badge, so you don't create duplicates. You can turn auto-add off anytime in Settings → General.

findReplace.title: Find & Replace
findReplace.hint: Type something to find across all tracks’ tags.

donateNudge.title: Your Surco so far
donateNudge.pitch: Surco is free and has no ads or tracking. If it keeps saving you time, a small donation helps keep it that way.

confirm.fillMessage_one: This reads the title, artist and track number from the file’s name and overwrites those fields in 1 track. Other tags are left untouched.
confirm.fillMessage_other: This reads the title, artist and track number from each file’s name and overwrites those fields in {{count}} tracks. Other tags are left untouched.
confirm.fillMessageFiltered_one: This reads the title, artist and track number from the file’s name and overwrites those fields in the 1 visible track. Tracks hidden by the filter are left alone.
confirm.fillMessageFiltered_other: This reads the title, artist and track number from each file’s name and overwrites those fields in the {{count}} visible tracks. Tracks hidden by the filter are left alone.
confirm.fillNone: None of the file names match a known pattern, so there is nothing to fill.
confirm.clearMessage_one: This removes the only track from the list. The file on disk is not deleted.
confirm.clearMessage_other: This removes all {{count}} tracks from the list. The files on disk are not deleted.
confirm.clearMessageFiltered_one: This removes the 1 visible track from the list — tracks hidden by the filter stay. The file on disk is not deleted.
confirm.clearMessageFiltered_other: This removes the {{count}} visible tracks from the list — tracks hidden by the filter stay. The files on disk are not deleted.
confirm.trashTitleWin_one: Move file to the Recycle Bin?
confirm.trashTitleWin_other: Move {{count}} files to the Recycle Bin?
confirm.trashMessage_one: This moves “{{name}}” to the Trash and removes it from the list. You can restore it from the Trash.
confirm.trashMessage_other: This moves {{count}} files to the Trash and removes them from the list. You can restore them from the Trash.
confirm.trashMessageWin_one: This moves “{{name}}” to the Recycle Bin and removes it from the list. You can restore it from there.
confirm.trashMessageWin_other: This moves {{count}} files to the Recycle Bin and removes them from the list. You can restore them from there.
confirm.deleteOriginalMessage: This moves the original “{{name}}” to the Trash. Your converted file stays in place. You can restore the original from the Trash.
confirm.deleteOriginalMessageWin: This moves the original “{{name}}” to the Recycle Bin. Your converted file stays in place. You can restore the original from there.
confirm.convertInPlaceMessage_one: Overwrite original is on: this rewrites the source file in place. The original is replaced and cannot be recovered.
confirm.convertInPlaceMessage_other: Overwrite original is on: this rewrites {{count}} source files in place. The originals are replaced and cannot be recovered.
